Context & Existing Rules

Internally, every order has a reference number in the format:
ORD-<5_digit_number> (example: ORD-01234)

Every order is associated with a Style Number, which is always stored in the format:
<style_number> - <style_description>
(example: ST102 - Athletic Crew Tee)

Required Change (Very Important)

I want to change only the way the Order Number is displayed and printed, without modifying how it is stored internally.

New Display Format

Wherever the Order Number is printed, listed, shown, exported, emailed, or displayed in the UI, it must appear as:

ORD-<style_number>-<5_digit_reference_number>

Example

Internal order reference: ORD-01234

Style number field value: ST102 - Athletic Crew Tee

Displayed Order Number should be:

ORD-ST102-01234

Scope of Change

Apply this display format consistently in all places, including but not limited to:

Order listing tables

Order detail pages

Admin dashboards

Emails and notifications

Invoices, PDFs, and exports

Logs or printable views (user-facing only)

Important Constraints

❌ Do NOT change the database schema

❌ Do NOT modify the internally stored order reference

✅ Only format the order number at the presentation / output layer

✅ Extract only the style number (text before -) from the style field

✅ Ensure backward compatibility for existing orders

**Reformatting the Order Number**

I'm currently focused on how to adjust the order number display. The current format, `ORD-<5_digit_number>`, needs to change to `ORD-<style_part>-<5_digit_number>`. I'm exploring various ways to integrate this change throughout the application in an efficient manner, minimizing disruption. I'm focusing on the best way to inject the new `<style_part>` into the display logic.


**Developing the Display Logic**

I've refined my approach to formatting the order numbers.  The crucial step is extracting the style number from the style field. I've pinpointed all instances of `order.order_no` within the application's UI, including various dashboard components and email templates. Now, I plan to build a dedicated helper function for consistent formatting, likely in `types.ts`.


**Constructing the Format Function**

I'm now implementing the `formatOrderNumber` function in `types.ts`. I started with the structure and ensured the logic handles the extraction of both the numeric and style parts, including edge cases like missing or differently formatted style numbers. I've considered potential null values too. I am ensuring that the implementation correctly constructs the final `ORD-<style_part>-<5_digit_number>` format. I'm writing tests to ensure this function is reliable.


**Mapping the Order Usage**

I'm presently mapping out all instances of the order number throughout the application, specifically focusing on how it's used beyond just display. I've pinpointed its use within modal props, print and receipt logic, and API calls for email sending. I've examined `MaterialsDashboard.tsx`, `QCDashboard.tsx`, and `SalesDashboard.tsx`, noting the diverse contexts of usage. My recent focus is now the `barcode_serial` generation, and how this relates to display.


**Integrating The Format Function**

I'm now fully immersed in the implementation. I've successfully added the `formatOrderNumber` function to `types.ts`, ensuring it handles cases with and without style numbers gracefully, including null values. I am updating files in the code base with the updated function as you advised. I've updated the `AdminDashboard.tsx`, `SubunitDashboard.tsx`, `MaterialsDashboard.tsx` and `QCDashboard.tsx`, and I'm turning my attention to the backend. Next steps are updating the backend email functionalities.


**Finalizing The Implementation**

I'm presently building the `formatOrderNumber` function, focusing on the dynamic `style_number` changes within order edits. I'm ensuring it extracts the numeric and style parts, handling missing or null `style_number` values gracefully. I have written the code in the manner I determined based on past experiences. I am now updating frontend pages and backend email handlers to utilize this new format. This includes the `AdminDashboard`, `SubunitDashboard`, and email sending functions, for consistent display. I'm making sure to address all parts that use the `order_no` format and update the style dynamically during edits.


**Adapting the Order Search**

I'm now making sure the search functionality accounts for the new `ORD-[STYLE_ID]-[SERIAL]` format, checking against the internal number *and* the formatted display to improve search accuracy and user experience. This involves updating the search logic in the `AdminDashboard.tsx` and the `SubunitDashboard.tsx`, as the user will search based on the updated number. Also, I'm ensuring all printed job sheets, receipts, and system-generated emails use the updated format.


**Updating the Search Functionality**

I'm now completely focused on updating the search feature. I've updated the logic in various dashboards, and I am making sure the search function is robust and handles the newly formatted order numbers effectively. I'm focusing on the `AdminDashboard.tsx`, as I know this is a core component. The search will now consider the new format `ORD-[STYLE_ID]-[SERIAL]` and support users searching based on either the style id or the serial number.

“I need you to create a new page in this app for the below use,
for every style most of the information remain the same so I would like to create a database which should represent the information for each style.
I have categorized the details into 4 major categories. Maintain it.
if you had any new ideas please add it. And have option to add new topics under each catergory and I wanted a page or a table like format which showcase all these across all order when I click it it should expand and show information.
and for each order I need a print button which will print all details along with attached Photos expanded and printed.
for each deatil I wanted some text as well as some attachments .
I should be able to edit any information in the database add or remove any attachments.
for each detail allow 0 or one or more attachments .

I also wanted 2 veiws for this page one which showcases entire dtabase I can scroll through and another a empty page which I can search slect and the selected style should be expalnded all information along with the attachments opened should be shown. I should also be able to open multiple styles in multiple view and compare across style.
for each option while creating or editing I need a button which says copy from x style. Which on clicked should show all exiting style and form the selected style the information should be copied.

General info 
Style no
Style text
Category : Casuals / Lite / Sportz
Type of packing : pouch/cover
no of pieces / box

Pre Production Requirements & INFO
Fabric
Colours & size
Measurement chart

Requirements during production
Elastic
Fusing sticker(size sticker & logo sticker)
Cone
Rope
Zipper
Stiching info

Packing Requirements
Photo tag
Tag
Feature tag
Hook
Loop 
Inner board 
Plastic cover/ pouch 
Size sticker
Box

For this create a sperate table and please give me the supabase sql prompt to do the same.”

**Establishing the Foundation**

I'm now focusing on a new "Style Database" or "Master Styles" feature. The central idea is to store reusable garment style information. I'm aiming to streamline production and packing data entry for new orders, preventing repeated inputs. I am trying to determine the scope of this database.


**Developing the Architecture**

I've been mapping out the data structure, with four core categories: General Info, Pre-Production, Production, and Packing. Flexibility is key; users can add new topics within these categories, and attach text, images, and documents to each. I'm also planning an expandable table view for browsing all styles, and a search/comparison view for side-by-side analysis, including printing with expanded images. I have a copy feature in mind, too.


**Refining the Data Model**

I've been iterating on the data model. I've abandoned a purely JSONB approach for the style details.  Instead, I'm leaning toward a hybrid structure with a main `style_library` table for core data and a related `style_metadata` table for flexible "add new topics". I'm also thinking of a two-tab interface: a grid/scrollable library and a search/compare "Lab." I want to restrict access to admins or users with a specific role via a new "Style Lab" sidebar icon.


**Designing the Database Structure**

I'm now implementing the SQL schema for the style database. I'm building two primary tables: `style_library` for core garment info (like style number, category, packing type, etc.) and `style_details` for flexible metadata (general info, pre-production, etc.). The latter will allow attaching text, images, and documents. I've defined the types and added database functions for this functionality. I've also incorporated a copy logic, which enables users to select a source style and populate the current one when creating/editing.


**Enhancing the Interface**

I'm currently working on the user interface, prioritizing a dynamic "add custom field" feature within each section.  I'm also maintaining consistency with the Indigo/Slate theme and designing a card-based layout for the comparison view.  My goal is high-contrast labels on print sheets, and I've started on print logic to open a new window for full-resolution image rendering.

This is a sophisticated addition to the system. To implement this "Style Master Database" (let's call it the **Style Lab**), we need a flexible schema that handles the hierarchical nature of your data while allowing for custom fields and multiple attachments per field.

### Part 1: Supabase SQL Schema
Run this in your Supabase SQL Editor. It creates a master table for the styles and a details table that stores the specific requirements with JSONB support for multiple attachments.

```sql
-- 1. Create the Master Style table
CREATE TABLE style_library (
  id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
  style_no TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  style_text TEXT,
  category TEXT CHECK (category IN ('Casuals', 'Lite', 'Sportz')),
  packing_type TEXT, -- pouch / cover
  pcs_per_box INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- 2. Create the Detailed Requirements table
-- This stores the 4 categories and their dynamic topics
CREATE TABLE style_details (
  id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
  style_id UUID REFERENCES style_library(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  section TEXT NOT NULL, -- e.g., 'Pre Production', 'Production', 'Packing'
  label TEXT NOT NULL,   -- e.g., 'Fabric', 'Elastic', 'Photo Tag'
  content_text TEXT,
  attachments JSONB DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb, -- Array of {name, url, type}
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Index for faster lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_style_details_style_id ON style_details(style_id);

-- Enable Realtime
ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE style_library;
ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE style_details;
```

### Part 2: Code Implementation

I will now update the `types.ts`, `services/db.ts`, and create the new `StyleDatabase.tsx` page. I'll also update the navigation.

entries.description: Added StyleLibrary and StyleDetail interfaces to support the new Master Style database.
Added database functions for Style Library management (Fetch, Create, Update, Delete, and cloning).

Created a comprehensive Style Lab page with two views (Library and Comparison), expandable details, custom topics, multi-attachment support, cloning functionality, and specialized high-fidelity printing.
Added the "Style Lab" navigation item to the sidebar for Admin users.
Registered the new Style Database route.

-- 1. Enable Cascading Deletes

-- all related rows in style_details are automatically deleted by the database.
ALTER TABLE style_details

ALTER TABLE style_details
ADD CONSTRAINT style_details_style_id_fkey
FOREIGN KEY (style_id)
REFERENCES style_library(id)
ON DELETE CASCADE;

END $$;
